# Sage

> A cognition protocol for AI coding agents: a single AGENTS.md plus a folder of Markdown knowledge (agents/sage/). No install, no server, no Python, no MCP. Any agent that reads AGENTS.md (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Copilot) is forced to understand the system — team rules, blast radius, what to reuse — BEFORE writing code, and to return a proceed/warn/ask/reject verdict.

Knowledge is passive; cognition must be enforced. Instead of a service to deploy, Sage is one declarative file you copy into your repo and edit as Markdown. Share it with `git push`.

## Install

- Copy the protocol to your repo root: `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qorstack/sage/main/AGENTS.md -o AGENTS.md`
- Optional starter knowledge: copy the `agents/` folder from the repo (or let it fill in as the agent captures rules).
- Commit both. Every agent that reads AGENTS.md now follows the protocol.

## How it works

- Pipeline before any code change: name the intent, read `agents/sage/<domain>/`, reuse before creating, assess impact/risk, emit a `Risk:` / `Decision:` header, stop on `ask`/`reject`.
- Knowledge = Markdown with YAML frontmatter under `agents/sage/<domain>/` (`rules.md`, `decisions/<slug>.md`): id, type, title, domain, tags, status, enforcement, applies_to, source.
- Learns from conversation: the agent captures rules/corrections you state as `status: proposed`; you approve by editing to `status: approved`.
- Enforcement: `block` (refuse violations) | `warn` (flag) | `advise` (strong default).

## Docs

- AGENTS.md — the full protocol; this file IS the product.
- agents/sage/ — starter cognition rules by domain (auth, payment, webhook, order, otp, notification, worker).
